pr.title: Change default max errors to 0 (unlimited) in `configure.py`
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Updated the repository configuration script so the compiler max-errors option defaults to 0, documented in the help text as meaning unlimited, instead of stopping after 1 error by default.
what_changed: In `configure.py`, the `--max-errors` argument default changed from `1` to `0`.
The argparse help text for `--max-errors` changed from saying default 1 to saying default 0, meaning unlimited.
The PR was a small one-file change with 2 added and 2 deleted lines.
smart_moves: Kept the CLI help string synchronized with the new default value, avoiding stale or misleading configuration documentation.
Used the existing `--max-errors` option rather than adding a new flag, preserving the build script interface while changing the default behavior.
Made unlimited diagnostics the default, which is useful for decompilation work where seeing all compiler errors at once can reduce iteration time.
decomp_lessons: For build and configure tooling, defaults should favor decompilation iteration: unlimited compiler errors can expose all current issues instead of requiring repeated rebuilds after each first error.
When changing an argparse default, update the help text in the same patch so future contributors can discover the behavior directly from `configure.py --help`.
Small developer-tooling changes can materially improve contributor workflow even when they do not affect game code matching.
naming_conventions: The existing CLI option name `--max-errors` was retained.
The help text explicitly documents sentinel value semantics: `0` means unlimited.
follow_up_queries: Find other configure.py argparse defaults whose help text may be stale or ambiguous.
Search for downstream build scripts or documentation that mention --max-errors defaulting to 1.
Check whether compiler invocation code treats max-errors 0 consistently as unlimited.
